commit.message:
fix(mcp): silence ctx-hub startup output

Summary:
- make skill registration quiet by default during MCP startup
- add a regression test for silent registerSkillTools execution
- normalize manifest input types for interface reference generation

Rationale:
- ctx-hub was writing to stdout before initialize, which breaks the
  stdio MCP handshake
- optional logging keeps startup diagnostics available without
  polluting MCP responses

Tests:
- node --test --test-concurrency=1 tests/skill-contracts.test.mjs
  tests/mcp-tool-registry.test.mjs tests/interface-reference.test.mjs
- manual smoke: launch node scripts/ctx-mcp-hub.js with stdout/stderr
  capture and verify stdout stays empty

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): honor DAO RPC ordering in wagmi config (#560)

Summary:
- normalize DAO chain metadata in a shared helper reused by the dapp
  provider
- build explicit viem transports from DAO-configured RPC URLs, keep
  their fallback order stable, and fail fast when none are configured
- avoid adding the built-in mainnet chain when the DAO itself already
  runs on chain id 1

Rationale:
- duplicate mainnet entries let RainbowKit and wagmi prefer built-in
  Ethereum RPC defaults over daoConfig.chain.rpcs
- explicit fallback transports preserve DAO RPC priority and fail over
  to the next configured endpoint when one RPC is unavailable
- empty RPC lists should fail with a clear DAO-chain error instead of
  deferring to an unusable transport with no URL

Tests:
- pnpm exec eslint src/config/wagmi.ts src/providers/dapp.provider.tsx
  src/components/members-table/hooks/useMembersData.ts
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
- pnpm dlx tsx -e ordered fallback transport probe
- pnpm dlx tsx -e empty RPC error probe
- curl -fsSL https://api.degov.ai/dao/config/fluence-dao?format=yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore multicall batching for DAO RPC reads (#559)

* fix(web): restore multicall batching for RPC reads

Summary:
- fix the shared multicall batch size to use viem's byte-based default
- configure wagmi transports from each chain's DAO RPC list via fallback
- make shared wagmi multicall batching explicit for public read traffic

Rationale:
- the previous batch size of 10 bytes split existing multicall reads into
  near-single-call requests
- RainbowKit's default transport setup only created one http transport per
  chain, which skipped the DAO RPC fallback list from config
- reducing fan-out at the shared client layer lowers pressure on public RPCs
  without rewriting each page-level hook

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
(cherry picked from commit cb724fb8759aa7ef9421e4e117be0313dfc6ee8c)

* fix(web): harden wagmi transport selection

Summary:
- preserve DAO RPC ordering by disabling viem fallback ranking
- dedupe configured chains and transports by chain id before creating wagmi
  config
- fail fast when a chain has no configured RPC URLs

Rationale:
- automated review surfaced edge cases that could silently reorder RPC
  selection or duplicate mainnet entries
- raising an explicit config error is easier to diagnose than a later
  request-time transport failure

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(test-e2e): add runtime assertion primitives and testing framework (#15)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added runtime assertion primitives (assert, assert_eq) that report
failures and abort.
* New end-to-end test runner that produces JSON reports and
human-readable summaries.

* **Tests**
* Many E2E cases added: basic passing suites (arithmetic, functions,
bindings, strings, hello world) and failing suites (compile errors,
runtime assertion failures) with expected output fixtures.

* **Chores**
* E2E suites integrated into the build with a toggle to enable/disable
them.

* **Documentation**
* CI and README updated to document skipping E2E on Windows and how to
disable E2E.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: extract load_pretrain_weights + apply_lora into models/weights.py (#840)

Eliminates duplicated weight-loading and LoRA application logic between
detr.py (_load_pretrain_weights_into, _apply_lora_to) and module_model.py
(_load_pretrain_weights, _apply_lora).  The canonical unified implementation
lives in the new src/rfdetr/models/weights.py and both callers delegate to it.

Key changes:
- New src/rfdetr/models/weights.py: load_pretrain_weights() uses the more
  complete module_model.py logic (Pydantic model_fields_set for user-override
  detection, auto-align to fine-tuned checkpoints, expand-back for explicit
  num_classes) plus class_names extraction from detr.py.  apply_lora() is
  the unified LoRA application function.
- detr.py: removes ~80 lines of private weight/LoRA methods; delegates to
  weights.py; fixes mutable COCO_CLASS_NAMES returned directly from
  class_names property (now returns list copy).
- module_model.py: removes ~95 lines of private weight/LoRA methods;
  delegates to weights.py.
- tests/models/test_weights.py: 8 new characterization tests (4 reinit
  scenarios, 2 class_names extraction, 2 LoRA application).
- test_load_pretrain_weights.py, test_module_model.py, test_detr_shim.py:
  migrated patch targets and call sites to rfdetr.models.weights.
- Pin torchvision>=0.17.0 (aligns with torch>=2.2.0 floor)
- Pin pydantic>=2.0,<3 (formalises existing v2-only API usage)
- Move peft from core deps to [lora] and [train] optional extras
- Replace top-level `from peft import PeftModel` in backbone.py with
  lazy try/except import inside Backbone.export()
- Fix merge_and_unload() result not being assigned back to self.encoder
- Add ImportError hints in _apply_lora_to() and _apply_lora() pointing
  users to `pip install rfdetr[lora]`
- Add characterization test: importing backbone must not pull peft into
  sys.modules
- Add regression test: Backbone.export() replaces encoder with merged model
- `_RemovedModuleFinder.find_spec` now short-circuits on any `fullname`
  that does not start with `rfdetr.`, preventing bare top-level imports
  such as `import util` or `import deploy` from being hijacked by the
  RF-DETR migration finder after `import rfdetr`.
- Replace the type-identity dedup guard with a `sys`-level sentinel
  (`sys._rfdetr_removed_finder`) so `importlib.reload(rfdetr)` never
  inserts a second finder into `sys.meta_path`.
- Add two characterization tests: scope guard (bare names return None)
  and reload idempotency (finder count unchanged after reload).

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

commit.message:
Phase 3B: Channel system — event routing, transports, push notifications

Event router (event_router.rs):
- Route events by EventFilter to multiple targets
- Target types: McpChannel, FileNotification, Webhook, AgentStdin
- Auto-cleanup on agent exit (remove_session)

Transports (transports.rs):
- McpChannelTransport: JSON format for Claude Code MCP channel capability
- FileTransport: JSONL append with timestamps
- WebhookTransport: async HTTP POST with timeout
- StdinTransport: human-readable notification via daemon client

Formatters (formatters.rs):
- Human-readable notifications for StatusChange/AgentExited/TaskUpdate/ContextWarning/LoopDetected
- JSON serialization for programmatic consumption

Channel binary (main.rs):
- CLI args with clap (--socket, --config)
- Connects to daemon, subscribes to all events
- Routes and pushes via configured transports
- TOML config support for default routes and webhooks

788 LOC channel, 67 tests total, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Make the adoption path obvious for new readers

The public README already documented the reusable bootstrap and skills, but a first-time reader still had to scan the page to figure out the fastest path to using it in their own repository.

Add a short copy-paste adoption section near the top with the bootstrap command and the minimal follow-up needed for extra co-authors.

Constraint: Keep the quick-start addition short and avoid re-explaining details already covered later in the README
Rejected: Replace the fuller reuse section | the quick block is complementary, not a substitute for the detailed notes
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the top-of-file adoption guidance copy-paste friendly; move edge cases to the detailed reuse section below
Tested: reviewed the updated README excerpt locally for clarity and command accuracy
Not-tested: External social preview rendering

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Surface CI status directly in the public README

The repository already had a working verification workflow, but readers landing on the public page had no immediate signal that the bundle was currently passing its self-test.

Add a GitHub Actions status badge near the top of the README and link it to the workflow page.

Constraint: Keep the README change small and avoid duplicating workflow details already documented below
Rejected: Add a separate CI section at the top | the badge is a lighter, more standard signal for repository health
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the badge URL aligned with the workflow filename and main branch if the workflow is renamed later
Tested: fetched the badge URL and confirmed an HTTP 200 response; checked the updated README header locally
Not-tested: Visual rendering differences outside GitHub

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Keep the public sample repo self-verifying

The repository had become public and reusable, but it still relied on manual local checks to prove that the attribution bundle actually worked after future edits.

Add a reusable verification script plus a GitHub Actions workflow that bootstraps the bundle into a fresh temporary Git repository and confirms a generated commit receives the Codex co-author trailer.

Constraint: The CI check must validate real Git behavior, not just file presence
Rejected: Lint-only workflow | it would miss regressions in bootstrap or commit-message injection behavior
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep CI aligned with the local verification script so repository and workflow checks do the same end-to-end validation
Tested: bash -n on the new verification script; ran the full local verification flow successfully; confirmed actions/checkout latest release is v6.0.2 before pinning the workflow to @v6
Not-tested: Execution of the new workflow inside GitHub Actions after push

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make the sample attribution setup reusable across repositories

The sample repository had become a good reference, but using it in another project still required manually copying files and remembering which pieces belonged together.

Add a bootstrap script that copies the managed hook, setup script, and repo-local Codex skills into another existing Git repository, then runs the target setup script automatically.

Constraint: Avoid maintaining duplicate template trees with the same hook and skill content
Rejected: Separate template directory with copied files | duplication would drift from the source of truth
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the bootstrap file list explicit so changes to managed attribution assets remain reviewable
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; bootstrap into a fresh temporary Git repo; verified copied files, local Git config, skill paths, and a test commit with the Codex co-author trailer
Not-tested: Overwrite flow with --force against an already customized target repo

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make attribution health checks one command away

The sample repository could install and manage co-author trailers, but it still lacked a quick read-only health check for confirming that the hook, config, and recent commits were aligned.

Add a third repo-local skill that prints the local hook path, commit template, configured ai.coauthor values, and the co-author trailers on the latest commit.

Constraint: Keep the status check read-only and repository-local
Rejected: Infer status from files alone | Git config and commit history are the actual source of truth
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep this skill diagnostic-only; do not let it mutate Git config or commit history
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; ran the status script and verified reported hook path, template, co-authors, and latest trailer
Not-tested: Skill rendering in Codex app or IDE surfaces

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make additional co-author trailers easy to manage

The repository could install the Codex hook, but it still required raw git config commands to add or fix other co-author entries.

Add a second repo-local skill that lists, adds, and removes ai.coauthor values so people can manage extra trailers without editing hook files or memorizing git config syntax.

Constraint: Do not guess provider emails that are not verified in this repo
Rejected: Hard-code Gemini and Claude presets | unsupported or unverified emails would create misleading defaults
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep provider-specific presets conservative; only bake in values that are verified and intentionally supported
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; add/list/remove cycle for a temporary Gemini-formatted entry; verified final ai.coauthor list returned to Codex only
Not-tested: Codex skill selection UX in every surface

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make Codex attribution setup self-serve inside the sample repo

The repository already demonstrated a deterministic Git hook, but it did not yet provide a Codex-native way to install or repair that setup.

Add a repo-local skill in .agents/skills that wraps the existing setup script so Codex can turn the hook on without relying on memory or ad hoc shell commands.

Constraint: Keep attribution deterministic and repository-local
Rejected: Codex-only skill without a Git hook | commit-time enforcement would remain non-deterministic
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the Git hook as the enforcement layer; the skill should only install or repair it
Tested: bash -n for the hook, setup script, and skill installer; ran the skill installer and verified local Git config
Not-tested: Codex app or IDE skill-chip rendering

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Phase 3A: Intelligence layer — context rotation, cross-review, auto-recovery

Context monitor (context_monitor.rs):
- Estimates context usage from agent output patterns + byte proxy
- Rotation threshold (default 80%), rotation prompt with task/progress/files/decisions
- Agent-specific max tokens (Claude 200K, Codex 192K, Gemini 1M)

Cross-review engine (review_engine.rs):
- Adversarial cross-model review pairs (Claude↔Codex, Gemini→Claude)
- Structured review prompt with 5 categories (correctness/perf/security/style/arch)
- Parser for VERDICT/ERROR/WARNING/NIT/SUGGESTED FIX format
- needs_changes: Error severity or RequestChanges blocks merge

Auto-recovery (recovery.rs):
- Decision tree: loop→restart, stuck→nudge→restart→escalate, error→retry→escalate
- Tailored nudge/restart prompts with context preservation
- Configurable thresholds (idle 5min, loop 5x, max 3 retries)

Integration:
- PtyManager: health_check(), trigger_rotation(), request_review()
- socket_server: RequestReview handler returns structured ReviewRequest

875 LOC intelligence, 58 tests total, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Update docs for telemetry and trip behavior

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add repo-level smoke and review workflow tests

Build a repo-level test harness that exercises the real harness binary,
add smoke coverage for the root CLI surface and idle status contract,
and add a golden review workflow E2E. Tighten status so finalize nodes do
not leak stale step-review metadata, and archive the approved plan with
durable closeout notes. Also capture a postmortem for the missed execute
workflow discipline and link the follow-up issue.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Phase 2B: MCP server (12 tools, JSON-RPC stdio) + task planner

MCP server (rist-mcp):
- main.rs: JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio transport, initialize/tools_list/tools_call
- tools.rs: 12 tool definitions with JSON Schema + daemon-backed handlers
  (spawn_agent, list_agents, get_agent_output, write_to_agent, kill_agent,
   archive_agent, wait_for_idle, run_command, read_task_graph, write_task_graph,
   get_file_ownership, merge_agent)
- MCP protocol: 2024-11-05, structuredContent support

Task planner (ristd):
- planner.rs: TaskPlanner with dependency-aware scheduling
  (ready_tasks, blocked_tasks, assign_task, stats, atomic save/load)
- Integrated into daemon socket_server for ReadTaskGraph/WriteTaskGraph

Daemon client extensions:
- spawn_agent_with_options, archive_agent, wait_for_idle, run_command
- read_task_graph, write_task_graph, preview_merge, merge_agent, get_file_ownership

35 tests passing, clippy clean, 858 LOC MCP + planner

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Phase 1B: TUI client with ratatui — sidebar, split panes, daemon client

- app.rs: Full app state (agents, focus, layout modes, input buffer, event handling)

- 1227 LOC TUI, clippy clean, all tests pass

commit.message:
Phase 1A review fixes: adapter trait, VecDeque ring buffer, event broadcast

- Add AgentAdapter trait + ClaudeCode/Codex/Gemini/Default implementations
- PtyManager now uses adapters to build real agent commands
- RingBuffer switched from Vec<u8> to VecDeque<u8> for O(1) drain
- drain_all() renamed to snapshot() (no mutation)
- check_exits() polls child processes, updates status/exit_code
- Event broadcast: StatusChange + AgentExited emitted on state transitions
- refresh_runtime_state() detects idle/working/stuck/waiting from output
- Loop detection (repeated line ×3)
- Deprecated flock warning suppressed
- 12 tests passing, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
meta: record shared-memory console-ring integration validation

This commit records completion/validation of the recent shared-memory and
console-ring integration work:

- native driver API now exposes shmem_create/shmem_map/shmem_unmap plus
  console_ring_id
- serial->framebuffer text forwarding moved from IPC text messages to a
  shared-memory console ring consumed by framebuffer_native
- WASM syscall surface extended with shmem_create/shmem_map/shmem_unmap
- shared-memory internals gained physical lookup and explicit retain/release
  ownership hooks
- framebuffer text IPC PUT_CHAR/PUT_STRING path removed in favor of ring drain
- README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md updated to reflect current behavior

Validation:
- cmake --build build --target run-qemu-test (pass)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Document the entrix hook file-length workflow and include copy-pasteable pre-commit config for other repositories.

feat(docs): add GitHub Pages deployment workflow

Summary:
- add a GitHub Actions workflow to build and deploy VitePress docs
  to GitHub Pages
- restrict automatic runs to pushes on main that touch docs files or
  the workflow itself
- set the VitePress base path from GitHub Actions metadata so asset
  URLs work under repository Pages paths

Rationale:
- publish docs automatically without running the workflow for every
  push or pull request
- keep the local docs experience unchanged while making Pages
  deployments work for project sites

Tests:

- GITHUB_ACTIONS=true GITHUB_REPOSITORY=wibus/synclax pnpm -C docs docs:build

repository.name: Synclax
repository.description: Turn tracker issues into controllable AI agent executions — with full observability, automatic lifecycle management.

commit.message:
Fix continuation retries and approval handling (#8)

* fix(orchestrator): refresh issue state before continuation retry

Summary:
- refresh the latest issue state from Linear before scheduling a
  continuation retry
- stop continuation retries when the refreshed issue has already moved to
  a non-active state such as Human Review
- add orchestrator tests that cover both the non-active stop path and the
  active continuation path

Rationale:
- prevent stale local state from causing extra continuation runs after an
  issue has already left the active workflow
- reduce unexpected token spend from repeated agent restarts on issues
  that have already been moved forward in Linear

Tests:
- go test ./pkg/symphony/orchestrator ./pkg/symphony/tracker/linear/...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(codex): use supported approval decisions

Summary:
- change app-server approval responses to use supported decision values
  instead of legacy reject-style variants
- add a regression test that exercises approval requests and verifies the
  emitted decision payload
- require local timestamps in workpad Notes and Confusions entries in the
  workflow policy

Rationale:
- prevent Codex app-server approval deserialization failures from blocking
  or derailing task execution
- make workpad progress notes easier to audit when debugging long-running
  attempts

Tests:
- go test ./pkg/symphony/codex ./pkg/symphony/orchestrator ./pkg/symphony/tracker/linear/...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: xxhx <xxhx@xxhxdeMacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: narrow monocular private save-path outcomes (#33)

* fix(monocular): narrow private save-path outcomes

Summary:
- add HEL-78 caller-side post-return diagnostics around monocular
  trajectory saves in the ORB-SLAM3 patch helper
- teach the monocular and private save-comparison follow-up parsers to
  distinguish skipped saves from after-return visibility outcomes
- extend focused regression tests for the new save-path evidence

Rationale:
- HEL-77 still conflated "save completed but file missing" with cases
  where System::SaveTrajectoryEuRoC never opened a file because no
  keyframes survived shutdown
- HEL-78 needs auditable evidence for whether the private frame
  trajectory is skipped before open, absent immediately after return,
  or removed later in shutdown

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_patch_orbslam3_baseline tests.test_run_monocular_baseline tests.test_run_private_save_comparison_followup
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/patch_orbslam3_baseline.py scripts/run_monocular_baseline.py scripts/run_private_save_comparison_followup.py
- make check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(progress): mirror delegate heartbeat in HEL-78 wrapper

Summary:
- mirror delegated private-run progress into the top-level HEL-78
  progress artifact while the save-comparison wrapper is active
- append wrapper heartbeat lines so the guarded orchestration log stays
  fresh during long ORB-SLAM3 rebuilds
- add regression coverage for invalid delegate artifacts and heartbeat
  payload mirroring

Rationale:
- the outer progress guard killed the HEL-78 wrapper after about ten
  minutes because only the delegated artifact/log were changing during
  the native build
- surfacing delegate heartbeats at the wrapper level keeps guarded
  reruns alive long enough to reach the actual private save diagnostics

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_run_private_save_comparison_followup
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/run_private_save_comparison_followup.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(progress): allow explicit identical build retries

Summary:
- pass an explicit identical-build retry override from the HEL-78 save
  comparison wrapper into the delegated private monocular runner
- log the delegate env override alongside the wrapper command so the
  rerun reason is auditable in the orchestration log
- add regression coverage for the wrapper's delegate retry override

Rationale:
- after the wrapper-heartbeat fix, the next HEL-78 rerun failed in
  build_orbslam3_baseline.sh because the prior interrupted build left an
  identical attempt signature recorded in .symphony/build-attempts
- codifying the explicit override in the wrapper keeps the rerun reason
  visible in repo code and avoids ad hoc shell state on the private host

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_run_private_save_comparison_followup
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/run_private_save_comparison_followup.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(diagnostics): expose HEL-78 reset-to-save state

Summary:
- instrument ORB-SLAM3 reset/save paths with HEL-78 atlas and
  pre/post-clear diagnostics
- surface the new reset/save evidence in monocular and private
  follow-up reports
- extend patcher and parser tests for the new diagnostic lines

Rationale:
- the prior rerun proved the save call completed but still left no
  trajectories, while the code path suggested ResetActiveMap was
  clearing the active map before shutdown
- making the reset and save state explicit turns that inference into
  auditable runtime evidence before another heavy rerun

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_patch_orbslam3_baseline tests.test_run_monocular_baseline tests.test_run_private_save_comparison_followup
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/patch_orbslam3_baseline.py scripts/run_monocular_baseline.py scripts/run_private_save_comparison_followup.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

  follow-up and save-comparison runners

  clean-host blocker in tracked follow-up docs

commit.message:
fix(monocular): codify HEL-74 shutdown save blocker (#29)

Summary:
- record the HEL-74 private ASan follow-up in repo docs and the final
  validation report
- teach the monocular baseline runner to summarize map creation,
  resets, save calls, and AddressSanitizer shutdown evidence from logs
- make the private follow-up report issue-aware and cover the new
  runtime parsing with tests

Rationale:
- the aggressive private lens-10 rerun no longer dies at first map
  initialization, so the checked-in blocker needed to move from the old
  post-init crash to the later shutdown/save boundary
- keeping the runtime evidence in scripts, docs, and tests leaves the
  next follow-up with auditable proof that trajectories are still
  missing after the save call reports completion

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_run_monocular_baseline
  tests.test_run_private_monocular_followup tests.test_final_validation_docs

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
